statusLine: EU fuel security remains precarious with 13 countries in critical status across multiple fuel types, despite overall reserves meeting minimum requirements.
fullAnalysis:
While EU-wide fuel reserves maintain adequate levels with petrol at 94.5 days, diesel at 95.8 days, and jet fuel at 89.9 days, the distribution reveals alarming disparities. Thirteen countries face critical shortages in at least one fuel type, with Romania showing the most severe deficits across all categories—petrol reserves at just 43.6 days and diesel at a dangerously low 20.2 days, both far below the mandatory 90-day minimum.

The crisis is particularly acute for jet fuel, where only Belgium maintains watch-level reserves at 97.6 days while all other reporting countries fall into critical or warning categories. Eastern European nations appear most vulnerable, with Poland, Lithuania, Latvia, and Slovakia all showing multiple critical fuel shortages. This geographic concentration of shortages creates significant supply chain risks for the region.

Oil markets reflect mounting pressure with Brent crude surging 6.41% to $107.64 per barrel, contributing to elevated fuel prices across the EU. Average petrol prices at €1.789 per liter and diesel at €2.00 per liter show the economic impact, with Netherlands facing the highest petrol costs at €2.334 per liter. The 27% crude price increase since the Middle East conflict began, combined with recent strategic reserve releases, indicates ongoing supply disruptions that could further strain already depleted national stockpiles.
keyPoints: Romania faces the most severe crisis with diesel reserves at only 20.2 days, less than 25% of the required minimum
Jet fuel shortages are widespread—every country except Belgium is in critical or warning status
Eastern European countries show concentrated vulnerability with multiple critical shortages across Poland, Lithuania, Latvia, and Slovakia
Brent crude oil has surged 27% since the Middle East conflict began, now at $107.64 per barrel with a 6.41% daily increase
Despite EU-wide averages meeting requirements, 13 countries face critical shortages in at least one fuel type, creating significant regional supply risks
